mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 cash flows and generating positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing equity investors to price in lower future earnings and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer price inflation creates stagflationary pressure that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields positive 20-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power when tested against single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: An expanding index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and corporate revenue growth, which directly supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations following INDPRO increases, or the signal exhibits a negative Rank IC over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a sub-4 percent unemployment rate indicates a tight labor market that sustains consumer spending and corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 60-day returns in >55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation surpassing the 3.0 percent threshold erodes real corporate profit margins and increases policy uncertainty, prompting equity market participants to reduce risk exposure and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in >60% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0%, or the signal fails to achieve a positive Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts alongside positive GDP growth confirm robust aggregate demand and construction sector activity, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative 60-day returns in >55% of cases during recovery phases, or predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production indicate contracting manufacturing capacity and weakening supply-side fundamentals, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of triggered instances, or the signal exhibits no statistical significance in event-study analysis.
